name: brz
title: brz
summary: easy to use distributed version control system
description:
easy to use distributed version control system
Breezy is a distributed version control system designed to be easy to
use and intuitive, able to adapt to many file formats and workflows, reliable,
and easily extendable.
.
Out of the box, Breezy supports both the Git and Bazaar repository
formats.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use brz because easy to use distributed version control system.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use brz as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `brz command [ command_options ]` as documented upstream for brz.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `brz help` as documented upstream for brz.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `brz help command` as documented upstream for brz.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair brz with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `brz --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
